{
  "name": "relay",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Local-first Codex-driven kanban desktop app.",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "electron-vite dev",
    "dev:logs": "electron-vite dev 2>&1 | tee /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build",
    "clean:dist": "node scripts/package-electron.mjs --clean",
    "package:binary": "node scripts/package-electron.mjs",
    "dist": "npm run clean:dist && npm run build && npm run package:binary",
    "logs": "mkdir -p \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay\" && touch \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\" && tail -n 200 -f \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\"",
    "logs:dev": "touch /tmp/relay-dev.log && tail -n 200 -f /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "test": "node tests/run-tests.mjs",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^10.0.0",
    "@dnd-kit/utilities": "^3.2.2",

    "react": "^19.0.0",

    "ulid": "^2.3.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^22.10.2",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.2",

    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.4",
    "electron": "^33.2.1",
    "electron-vite": "^2.3.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.2",
    "vite": "^5.4.11"
  }
}

The regression is in the flex/scroll containment rather than Markdown rendering. I’m going to add an explicit expanded-preview state to the primary modal column, make that state use bounded flex regions so the update composer occupies its own visible row, and add a focused style regression assertion to the existing renderer UI tests.
The code change is in place. I’m running the existing UI test bundle first so the new regression assertion is exercised with the same test runner as the rest of the renderer tests.

The full repo test runner passes; it emitted the existing esbuild warning about `import.meta` in a CJS test bundle, but no failures. I’m running the TypeScript check next because the change touches `App.tsx`.

**Summary**
Implemented a focused layout fix for the expanded ticket Markdown preview. The ticket detail primary column now gets an expanded-preview state, constrains its own overflow, lets the preview consume remaining space with `min-height: 0`, and keeps the update composer as a separate non-shrinking visible region.

**Files Changed**
- [src/renderer/src/App.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/App.tsx:3646)
- [src/renderer/src/styles.css](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/styles.css:2565)
- [tests/ticket-draft-ui.test.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/ticket-draft-ui.test.tsx:241)

**Commands Run**
- `grep` / `find` / `sed` / `nl` for local code inspection
- `git status --short`
- `git diff -- src/renderer/src/App.tsx src/renderer/src/styles.css tests/ticket-draft-ui.test.tsx`
- `npm test -- --runInBand`
- `npm run typecheck`

**Tests**
- `npm test -- --runInBand`: passed, 154 tests. Existing esbuild warning about `import.meta` in CJS bundle was emitted.
- `npm run typecheck`: passed.

**Subagent Usage**
None used.

**Remaining Risks / Follow-Up**
No known implementation blockers. I did not run a browser/manual visual validation in the Electron UI; coverage was added as a focused renderer style regression test. Existing `.relay` metadata changes were already present or generated by the run and were left untouched.
